meta.title: CHASSIS — Work, proven
meta.description: CHASSIS executes specialized work, proves every result before delivering it, and improves week after week. Billed per validated unit.
hero.sub: CHASSIS executes your profession's specialized work, proves every result before delivering it, and memorizes only what has been validated. Its success rate climbs week after week — and you only pay for what is proven.
hero.rule1l: every output passes the harness before delivery
hero.rule2l: learns only from the validated — never from noise
hero.rule3l: the verified success rate, your only KPI
hero.rule4l: billed per proven unit, not per license
principle.h2:
A system that refuses
what it cannot prove.
principle.sub: AI assistants promise. CHASSIS demonstrates. Every task enters with its verification criterion — without one, it is returned to a human. That discipline is what makes autonomy possible.
principle.c1h: The harness is the only gate
principle.c1p: Nothing is delivered, nothing is memorized, nothing is billed without a verdict. Every output is evaluated rule by rule — rules declared by your profession, versioned, auditable.
principle.c2p: Every validated fix, every real rejection becomes a permanent asset — named, versioned, revocable. Memory contains only the proven: it doesn't degrade, it compounds.
principle.c3p: The system replays your history and measures its predictive reliability. Below the threshold, it observes in silence. It has no right to propose what it cannot judge.
principle.c4p: Autonomy rises category by category, when the verified rate exceeds the threshold — never all at once. You keep your hand on every notch.
principle.c5p: Claude, GPT, local model: routed by task, cost and latency. The engine changes; the chassis — your rules, your memory — remains.
loop.sub: For every case, the same cycle — and every real-world verdict (the institution accepts, rejects) comes back to recalibrate the system. The loop is closed by the world, not by an opinion.
loop.s1p: The case enters with its verification criterion. Relevant memory tokens are recalled.
loop.s2p: The best-routed engine produces the candidate output, guided by past validated work.
loop.s3p: The harness judges, rule by rule. Line-by-line justification, never a black box.
loop.s4p: Compliant → ready. Anomaly → fix proposed. Unverifiable → returned to a human.
loop.s5p: According to the category's autonomy notch. The harness remains the gate, autonomy or not.
loop.s6p: Only the validated enters memory. Every real rejection becomes a learned rule.
journey.h2:
From your history to autonomy,
at the pace of proof.
journey.j1h: You declare your ground truth. Once.
journey.j1p: CHASSIS plugs into your existing tools — no new work interface. You point the system at your history: past cases and their real verdicts. That's all it asks for.
journey.j2p: The system replays your history and predicts every known verdict. Until its reliability reaches the threshold, it proposes nothing. You watch the gauge climb.
journey.j3h: Your morning queue, sorted by proof.
journey.j3p: Cases arrive pre-judged: compliant with line-by-line justification, anomalies with a proposed fix, out-of-scope returned to a human. You batch-approve what is proven, you decide on the rest.
journey.j4h: Every real verdict makes it better.
journey.j4p: The institution accepts or rejects: the system recalibrates, every rejection becomes a learned rule — paid for once. The curve climbs, and that's what you show your leadership.
journey.j5h: Notch by notch, category by category.
journey.j5p: When a category sustains the verified threshold over time, the cockpit proposes auto-processing. You switch it on. Your time moves to the cases that deserve a human — and every newcomer inherits the full memory on day one.
model.h3:
No licenses. No hours.
The validated unit.
model.sub: A SaaS bills you whether it works or not. A contractor bills time spent. CHASSIS can only bill one thing: a unit of work that passed the verdict — because it is the only one able to prove it. If the curve doesn't climb, you don't pay for its promise: you pay nothing.
cta.h2:
Give us 50 historical cases.
We only bill what the system validates.
cta.sub: Domains with a verifiable verdict: payroll & filings, billing and rejections, compliance, regulatory classification. Calibration runs on your history — your data remains yours, and the memory learned on your cases belongs to you.
